entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: Data engineer - SAP HANA
entry.content:
Getting certified as a data engineer on SAP HANA Cloud can feel like a big step.  The content is deep, the exam is detailed, and time is always tight.

That’s exactly why we created this Partner Learning Accelerator.

In this 3-hour live session, you’ll walk through key topics like data modeling, provisioning, and exam structure. You’ll see real examples, ask questions in real time, and get practical tips that go beyond slides. Plus, we’ll close with a focused exam wrap-up so you know what to expect.

If you’re working with SAP HANA Cloud or planning to, this is a strong way to build confidence before the C_HAMOD certification.

The thing is, preparation becomes much easier when you know where to focus.

Join us and move one step closer to certification.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013386.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #SAPHANA #DataEngineering #SAPLearning #CloudSkills

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP - Q1 2026 Innobytes
entry.content:
This first quarter has set the tone for 2026, bringing in new ideas and innovations all around Data, Joule and more. Let’s dive into some of the highlights Q1 has brought us.

 

 

AI & Data Foundation Highlight

Discovery and Data Agent in SAP HANA Cloud

The new Discovery and Data Agents are lowering learning barriers for developers and data users who just need answers. These new agents make it easier to explore, analyze, and act on data without needing deep knowledge of the underlying artifacts.

To see this demo in action, watch the video (starting at 0:11).

Learn more

  • Discovery & Data Agent on SAP Community
  • Register for the SAP HANA Cloud Data & Discovery Agents - Beta Program

 

Integration Highlight

Fresh Connections in SAP Integration Suite

New adapters for SAP Integration Suite on SAP BTP help transform your integration landscape, breaking down data silos, streamlining operations, and allow rapid adoption of new capabilities to innovate faster. 

The latest release offers:

  • The BambooHR adapter enables receiving and processing employee lifecycle data effectively.
  • The Microsoft Outlook Sender and Receiver adapter allows seamless integration for calendars, mail, attachments, and messaging directly into your mission-critical workflows. 

For Edge Integration Cella:

  • The File adapter, enabling secure file exchange with NFS systems
  • The OFTP2 adapter for compliant B2B connections to securely exchange messages from business partners. 

See the new integration content (starting at 2:14).

Learn more:

  • Integration Adapter
  • SAP Integration Suite on SAP Community

 

Agent & Application Development Highlights

API based Action Error Handling

In real-world automation, failures are inevitable. What matters is how the system handles them. This innovation brings precision and predictability to API error handling in SAP Build Process Automation on SAP BTP. When configuring an API-based Action step, enable error handling and define specific errors, handling authentication failures precisely. You can create generic categories, such as 4XX or 5XX, handling broader client or server issues.

The inability to define category overlap eliminates ambiguity, and deterministic runtime behavior is guaranteed. When an error occurs, precedence is strictly enforced:

  • Specific errors first
  • Generic category second
  • Undefined fallback last

At the Action level, you can configure retries that define the number of attempts and the delay between them. Afterwards, the error handling logic is executed. Every stage is fully logged in Monitoring, ensuring complete traceability and turning error handling from a black box into a controlled, observable workflow decision.

Watch the demo (starting at 3:35).

 

Conversational Work in SAP Build Work Zone 

There is now a powerful new way to work in SAP Build Work Zone. Save time by simply asking Joule what you need instead of clicking through multiple pages.

In the demo (starting at 5:33), a new project workspace for a cross-functional initiative is set up through a short conversation with Joule, enabling easy navigation, no technical steps, and only one screen. SAP Build Work Zone becomes more conversational, more intuitive, and more aligned with how people actually work.

Learn more:

  • Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

 

Core Platform Highlight

SAP Automation Pilot and Joule: Unlocking AI-Powered Ops Agents

The SAP Automation Pilot and Joule turn repeatable operational tasks into intelligent, agent-driven capabilities on SAP BTP.

By starting with a real operational process like a S/4HANA health check, use Automation Pilot’s low-code/no-code interface. Here, the necessary steps, inputs and validations can be collected and grouped into a Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server. The MCP Server exposes these automations as governed, reusable tools that can be safely consumed by AI agents without writing custom APIs.

Once published, connect it to Joule Studio to build a Joule Ops Agent that understands when and how to invoke these tools. The Ops Agent can now detect issues or fetch system status, triggering the right automation behind the scenes.

The DevOps or Admin team can simply ask the Ops Agent in Joule to ‘check my HANA Cloud and App instances health and remediate common issues’ by using the MCP Server to execute these steps securely, consistently, and audibly.

For a demo, watch the video (starting at 7:35).

Learn more:

  • Blog Post: From Automation to Agentic Ops
  • Blog Post: Meet your Ops Agents

 

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU, and we would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • Unlocking SAP BTP: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in March
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud and Datasphere News in March

*** UPDATE: Added the March Feature Highlight Video ***

The minor news is that I was on vacation 🏝️ hence the late news blog for March 😉
but my colleagues did not stop delivering releases while I was out 🚀

Plenty of small but super useful features and enhancements being delivered with our two releases in March 🔥

My monthly top features video for March 2026 is a little late this time due to my vacation, but will follow on YouTube 📺 the next days on the usual
🔗  Top Features Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL3ZRUb1AKkpS8bwmAREXo78O2xccXzoiy
🔗  March Feature highlights: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IoFg1SadBY8

 

 

My top features in March

 

Currency Conversion

There are two updates with currency conversion:

  1. Shift for Currency Conversion
    In the analytic model, you can now control whether currency conversion applies a shift of the decimal points or not.
  2. Simplified Sharing of Views Containing Currency Conversion
    You can now simply share SQL views containing a currency conversion column to another space, without needing also to share all the supporting currency conversion entities too. Very convenient.

➡️  More information: Create a Currency Conversion Column in a Graphical View and Enabling Currency Conversion with TCUR* Tables and Views.

 

DAC Support for associated dimensions of fact data

When creating an association from a fact to a dimension, you can now choose to apply the dimension's data access controls directly to the fact data.

You can see any data access controls applied in this way in a subsection Applied via Dimensions under the list of Data Access Controls in the side panel.

➡️  More information.

 

Support Large Parquet Files for Object Store Targets

You can now enable to create large files in Replication Flows when replicating data to supported cloud object store targets using the Parquet file format. This creates fewer and larger files during the initial load.

➡️  More information.

 

Manage Replication Flow Runs via the Command Line

You can now manage Replication Flow runs via the Command Line interface (CLI), meaning you can run, stop, and obtain the status of replication flows via the command line.

➡️  More information.

 

Email Notification for Expiring Consent

You will now automatically receive an email notification before your authorization consent expires. This will help ensure your scheduled tasks will continue without interruption.

➡️  More information.

 

Define Z-ORDER COLUMNS While Running an Optimize Task

While running an optimize task for your local table (file), you can now define Z-order columns to co-locate the data by columns and therefore reduce the amount of data that needs to be read.

Z Ordering co-locates similar data in the same files, which allows for better file skipping and faster queries. The difference from partitioning is that Partitioning creates physical folders, which is good for low-cardinality data like a region, Boolean values or the likes. Z-Ordering optimizes the file layout within those folders based on high-cardinality data customer Ids, time stamps and the likes.

➡️  More information.

 

Data Lineage enhancement for the BW DTPs

A new link type (dashed line) has been added to the Impact and Lineage Analysis diagram for Data Transfer Processes (DTP) and their associated Transformations (TRFN) across all BW systems. You can gain complete data traceability control by visualizing how one or more transformations are connected to and used within DTPs, enabling better understanding of data flow and dependencies in your BW environment.

➡️  More information.

 

 

Webinar Series: The Next Era of Business Data

One session remaining on the great sessions of this series. In case you could not make it live, all sessions are recorded and I added the links to the recordings below.

In case you are interested to join the last session live, please feel free to
➡️ sign up now: SAP BDC: The Next Era of Business Data

🔹Jan 29 | Discover SAP Business Data Cloud in 2026 - recording

🔹Feb 05 | The Smart Path to SAP BW Modernization - recording

🔹Feb 19 | Empowering SAP Datasphere Users with SAP Business Data Cloud Innovations - recording

🔹Feb 26 | From Data to Decisions: Analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP BDC - recording

🔹Mar 12 | From Data Silos to Business Insight: The Power of BDC Connect - recording

🔹Mar 19 | An Architect's Blueprint for SAP Business Data Cloud as the Foundation for Enterprise AI - recording

🔹Mar 26 | Design. Govern. Deliver. Data Products - Data Product Studio - recording

🔹Apr 16 | Breaking Down the SAP BDC Pricing Model

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud: Expert-Guided Implementation Workshop Series
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud is a fully managed, in-memory cloud database service as part of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It delivers high-speed processing and real-time analytics, which is especially valuable for data-intensive applications. The platform supports multiple data models, including relational, graph, spatial, JSON, and document store, allowing developers to work with different types of data in one environment. It also includes built-in data virtualization and integration capabilities, enabling organizations to access and combine data from SAP and non-SAP sources.

SAP HANA Cloud provides a comprehensive environment for building intelligent, data-driven solutions. It supports advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive capabilities directly within the database, which reduces data movement and accelerates insight generation. Developers can use familiar tools and languages such as SQL, Java, Node.js, and Python to create applications and services.
The platform also includes data lake functionality for storing and processing large volumes of less-frequently accessed data at a lower cost, while still allowing seamless querying alongside in-memory data. Its multi-tier storage approach helps organizations balance performance and cost by automatically managing where data resides. Overall, SAP HANA Cloud enables organizations to modernize their data landscape, support real-time decision-making, and drive digital transformation initiatives.

 

🚀 Empower Your Data Journey: Introducing Our New SAP HANA Cloud Expert Guided Implementation Series 

In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, speed of transformation is a must. Businesses across the globe are evolving to make faster, smarter, and more connected decisions by building intelligent, data-driven solutions using advanced analytics, machine learning, and predictive capabilities. To support this evolution, we’re excited to announce our renewed series of Expert-Guided Implementation workshops (EGI) from SAP Enterprise Support designed to help you upskill, modernize, and future-proof your business processes. Whether you're looking to modernize legacy systems, harness the power of cloud data, or unlock real-time insights through analytics, this workshop series offers targeted enablement for every stage of your development. 

*The following EGI sessions will run throughout the year, with quarterly updates to the schedule. 
Each session is limited to 30 individual customer participants.  
**Registration links below direct to SAP Learning content for SAP Enterprise Support, which requires a one-time registration with a valid S-user.

Configuration, Administration, Security and Monitoring topics for SAP HANA Cloud

In this live EGI session we will guide you through the SAP HANA Cloud capabilities as DBaaS, demonstrate the key features available in the latest quarterly release of the tool and show the benefits of using the administration tools for DB Monitoring, security and configuration. For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP HANA Cloud
  • Overview of SAP BTP
  • Introduction and usage of Administration tools
    • SAP HANA Cloud Central
    • SAP HANA Cockpit
    • SAP HANA Database Explorer
  • Managing, Monitoring and Alert Notification in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Workload Management:
    • Managing Tables
    • HANA NSE
  • SAP HANA HDI Overview
  • Introduction and usage of Cloud Foundry CLI 
  • Security Administration and User management

Register Here* 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Modeling

In this live EGI session, we will guide you through the process of modeling in SAP HANA Cloud. After the completion of the session, you will have the knowledge of calculation view usage and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio. Also, we showcase the core capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud for calculation view graphical modelling and provide help on about SQL Script procedures and implementing functions using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP). For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Usage of SAP Business Application Studio and Administration tools
  • Deploy SAP Business Application Studio
  • Introduction and modeling Calculation views
  •  Modeling with SAP Business Application Studio and using Database Explorer
  • Modeling features
  • Generating restricted and calculated columns
  • SQL Script usage
  • Performance Analysis, Debug mode & SQL Analyser
  • Overview of Best Practices

 Register Here* 

 

SAP HANA Cloud Multiple Model Programming including Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph

In this live EGI session, we empower customers with the knowledge of SAP HANA Cloud multiple model programming using the latest concepts including vector engine, knowledge graph engine and retrieval augmented generation. After the completion of the workshop, customers will have the knowledge of multiple model engines and how to incorporate artificial intelligence and retrieval augmented generation capabilities.

Agenda: 

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Understand SAP HANA Cloud architecture and deployment options
  • Introduction to multiple model programming
  • Data Tiering considerations for multiple model programming
  • Overview of SAP Business Application Studio
  • Spatial data concepts in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Understanding spatial features and functions
  • Spatial data modeling and processing
  • Graph processing in SAP HANA Cloud
  • JSON document store in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Automated machine learning (AutoML) in SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP HANA Cloud vector database
  • Capabilities and benefits of multiple model processing with vectors
  • SAP HANA Cloud knowledge graph engine
  • Knowledge graph and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
  • Real-world applications for Retrieval Augmented Generation

Register Here* 

 

Pro-code cloud application programming development through SAP Build and AI (Joule)

In this live EGI session we will empower you with the knowledge of cloud application programming development in SAP HANA Cloud. After the completion of the session, you will have the knowledge of CAP CDS development and design in SAP HANA Cloud and understand how to build and deploying cloud application projects from scratch and with reduced costs utilizing pro-code development using SAP Build Code with Joule. For details, please read the abstract here.

Agenda:

  • Introduction to SAP BTP and SAP HANA Cloud
  • Introduction to SAP Build Code and Prompt Engineering
  • CAP Architecture and SAP Business Application Studio features
  • Build and deploy a CAP project using SAP Business Application Studio
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud using CAP CDS
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud Optimization
  • Building Application using SAP Build Code and Joule
  • Modeling in SAP HANA Cloud Management and Administration

Register Here* 

Learning Outcomes from the EGI Series:

  • Configuration, Administration, Security and Monitoring topics for SAP HANA Cloud:
    Master SAP HANA Cloud configuration, performance monitoring and secure user management using standard administration tools.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Modeling:
    Develop Calculation views and SQL Script procedures within SAP Business Application Studio.
  • SAP HANA Cloud Multiple Model Programming including Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph:
    Implement advanced Vector Engine, Knowledge Graph, and Spatial Data capabilities to power Generative AI and Retrieval Augmented Generation scenarios
  • Pro-code cloud application programming development through SAP Build and AI (Joule):
    Build and deploy end-to-end CAP applications efficiently using SAP Build Code and Joule


Who Should Attend? 

This workshop series is ideal for developers, modelers, business analysts, system administrators, database administrators and SAP customers looking to transform their business processes. Whether you're just getting started or want to go deeper into SAP's modern toolset, there’s a session tailored for your needs. 

 

GET MORE CONTENT LIKE THIS!

 SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps — your go-to team for Foundational Success Plan support and empowerment — have a dedicated SAP Community Group.

As part of your SAP cloud subscription, you get access to:

  • Expert-led resources
  • Tailored recommendations
  • Advisory services
  • …all designed to help you accelerate innovation and success.


👉 Visit our Community Group
Sign in with your S-user ID or Universal ID, then hit “Subscribe” to stay updated.

Looking to dive deeper into data-driven innovation?
Within the group, explore the Data & Analytics Value Map to connect with your Foundational Support team. Simply click “Join Group”, and once inside, you can even request a call with a topic expert to get personalized guidance and enablement support.

 

 

 

 

 

Quicklinks

  • SAP Business Data Cloud : Expert-Guided Implementation series
  • Ensuring a Smooth SAP Datasphere Deployment: The Value of Continuous Quality Checks
  • SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps - Community Group
  • SAP Enterprise Support - Guided Learning

entry.title: AGP’s Next Chapter: Dual SAP Certification Fuels Cloud Growth Ambitions
entry.content:
AGP, a specialized SAP service provider based in Chile, has reached an important milestone in its growth journey: achieving its first-ever certifications for SAP SuccessFactors HCM operations and SAP BTP operations. But for AGP, this achievement goes beyond formal recognition. It reflects a deliberate effort to strengthen quality, standardize processes, and further mature as an organization. In this interview, AGP shares what motivated them to pursue certification at this stage, how the audit process catalyzed internal improvement, and why they see certification as a foundation for long-term excellence rather than a one-time validation. The conversation also explores AGP’s expanding role in cloud transformation - building capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation - and outlines its vision to become a leading SAP partner in the region.

Could you start by telling us about your company – what services do you offer, and which industries or customer types do you primarily serve?

AGP is a specialized SAP service provider focused on implementing, supporting, and optimizing solutions such as SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP. We primarily work with large-scale organizations across industries such as healthcare, mining, transportation, and services, where operational quality and business continuity are critical. Our main differentiator is our team: highly committed professionals with international experience who work closely with our clients, understand their businesses, and act as strategic partners.

AGP team

 

You were recently certified for the first time for SAP SuccessFactors HCM operations and SAP BTP operations. What motivated you to pursue these certifications at this point in your journey?

The certification represents a natural step in our evolution. We have been strengthening our SAP operations with a strong focus on quality, processes, and specialization, and these certifications formally validate that path. Our main motivation was to reassure our clients that we operate under SAP-recognized standards, reinforcing our value proposition based on trust, excellence, and delivery capability.

 

Looking back, how did you experience the audit process? And did it give you any new perspectives – whether on your internal governance, quality standards, or ways of working?

The process was very enriching. It allowed us to confirm that our practices are aligned with international standards while also identifying opportunities for improvement in governance, documentation, and standardization. More than an audit, we experienced it as a step forward in organizational maturity, reinforcing our commitment to continuous improvement.

 

SAP is strongly focused on the cloud. How are you already supporting customers in their migration, and where do you plan to expand?

We are already supporting our customers in their transition to the cloud, especially SAP SuccessFactors and SAP BTP solutions. We participate in both implementation and AMS services, ensuring the solutions' continuity, evolution, and adoption. Our expansion focus is on strengthening capabilities in integration, extensibility, and automation on SAP BTP, as well as enabling more agile and scalable cloud operating models.

How do you plan to differentiate yourself and leverage the certification – both in the market and in your communication with customers?

Our differentiation goes beyond the certification itself - it lies in how we work. The certification supports a model based on close client relationships, high-level teams, and a strong service culture. We will leverage it to reinforce market trust, demonstrating that we combine SAP standards with a human, flexible, and results-oriented approach.

Where do you see the biggest growth opportunities, and how do you plan to scale your team to meet demand?

We see significant opportunities in cloud adoption, especially in talent management solutions and innovation platforms like SAP BTP. To scale, we are strongly investing in local talent development, training and certifying new consultants, while strengthening our team with senior profiles who bring experience and culture. We believe sustainable growth is achieved by building strong teams, not just increasing capacity.

What are your strategic plans to deepen your partnership with SAP in the coming years?

Our goal is to position ourselves as a leading partner in the region. We aim to continue expanding our certifications, strengthening our cloud capabilities, and co-innovating with SAP to deliver real value to our customers. Our relationship with SAP is strategic, and we see it as a long-term partnership built on trust, quality, and shared growth.

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners, as well as an overview of all certified partners, can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: End to End Currency Conversion Integration using SAP Integration Suite (SAP CPI)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Introduction

While learning SAP Integration Suite, I wanted to build a real‑world scenario instead of simple message mapping examples. During this process, I explored how external APIs can be integrated into CPI, and currency conversion felt like a practical and finance‑relevant use case.

SAP Integration Suite provides the perfect middleware capability to fetch exchange rates from external APIs, perform calculations, and return results in a clean, readable format.

In this blog, I have designed an end‑to‑end Currency Conversion I-Flow that:

✔ Accepts JSON data from a source system
✔ Calls an external exchange rate API
✔ Converts the currency
✔ Formats the response beautifully (HTML table)
✔ Delivers the final output via HTTPS/Mail

 

Overall Architecture with I-Flow Design

Below is the architecture I have used in this integration scenario:

Main Flow Steps​

  • HTTPS Sender – Receive JSON input​
  • JSON to XML Converter​
  • Content Modifier – Extract fields​
  • Request‑Reply – Call External Exchange RateAPI​
  • Groovy Script – Perform conversion & prepare HTML​
  • Mail Adapter – Send result email​
  • Exception Subprocess – Error output.

How I Designed the I-Flow

I designed the I-Flow using the following steps:

  1. HTTP Sender Adapter
    I exposed the I-Flow as an HTTP endpoint where the user can pass source currency, target currency, and amount as parameters.

  2. Content Modifier
    I used a Content Modifier to prepare request parameters and headers required by the RapidAPI currency conversion service.

  3. Request-Reply (HTTP Receiver)
    The external API call was configured directly in the HTTP receiver adapter. This step helped me understand how authentication headers and query parameters work in CPI.

  4. Groovy Script – Response Processing
    I wrote a Groovy script to parse the JSON response and extract:

    • Source currency
    • Target currency
    • Amount
    • Converted value Instead of returning plain JSON, I converted the output into a simple HTML table, which makes the response more readable when tested via browser or Postman.

  5. Exception Subprocess
    While testing, I encountered HTTP 500 and 429 errors. To handle this, I implemented an Exception Subprocess that captures:

    • Error message
    • HTTP response code
    • MPL ID
      This helped me clearly understand the importance of structured error handling in CPI.

Content Modifier Property

Initially, I tried passing query parameters directly, but it caused confusion during testing. Using a Content Modifier gave me better visibility and control over headers, especially while debugging RapidAPI authentication issues.

Groovy Script of conversion Logic and HTML Beautification

 This script is responsible for:

  • Reading the API response
  • Extracting the exchange rate
  • Reading the input currencies
  • Calculating the converted amount
  • Passing the result to the next step

This script prepares a beautiful HTML table for the final output. This improves readability when the response is sent to Postman, email, or UI.

Test the Flow Using Postman

I have used Postman to trigger the HTTPS endpoint.

 


This output will also trigger through the mail which I have mentioned in mail adapter.

Working of Exception Subprocess for Errors (Negative Tests)

Exception subprocess must contain:

  1. Exception Start Event
  2. Groovy Script – Prepare HTML Error Message
  3. End Message

The Exception Subprocess is triggered automatically by CPI whenever the I-Flow encounters an error.
Its job is to:

  • Capture the error
  • Identify the reason
  • Classify the error
  • Convert it into a readable HTML format
  • Return it to the end user (Postman / Email)

This receives a clean, well‑formatted message instead of a raw technical stack trace.

My Personal Experience and Learnings

While implementing this integration scenario in SAP Integration Suite, I encountered several real‑time challenges that helped me understand the platform more deeply.

One of the key learnings for me was how important proper exception handling is in CPI. Initially, I focused only on the happy path, but during testing with Postman, I started facing HTTP 500 and XPath errors. Designing a reusable Exception Subprocess helped me standardize error responses and made the I-Flow much more robust.

This blog was written with the assistance of generative AI tools for language refinement. The integration design, implementation steps, testing, and learnings are based on my personal hands‑on experience.

Conclusion

This end‑to‑end scenario demonstrates how SAP Integration Suite can integrate external APIs, enrich responses, perform computations, and return a polished output.
It is a perfect demonstration of:

  • Integration patterns
  • Error handling
  • Groovy scripting
  • API integration
  • Output formatting

You can extend this scenario to:

  • Multi-currency batch conversions
  • ERP finance integration
  • UI5 front-end
  • Microservices

entry.title: Get Certified: SAP Certified - SAP Generative AI Developer
entry.content:
Getting certified in Generative AI is no longer a “nice to have”.  It is quickly becoming part of your daily toolkit as an SAP Partner.

This live “Get Certified” series is designed to guide you step by step toward the SAP Certified – SAP Generative AI Developer credential.  You will start with a clear kickoff session, then move into focused content reviews with real demos.  Think prompt design, LLM fundamentals, and hands-on work in SAP Generative AI Hub.

The thing is, preparing alone can feel overwhelming.  These sessions give you direct access to experts who walk you through key topics and answer your questions in real time.  That makes a big difference when you are trying to connect theory with real project scenarios.

You also get a final wrap-up session to sharpen your readiness before the exam.

If this certification is on your roadmap, this is a structured way to get there with confidence.

Register now.  https://learning.sap.com/live-sessions/get-certified-sap-certified-sap-generative-ai-developer

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #GenerativeAI #SAPLearning #AITraining

entry.title: Partner Learning Accelerator: SAP certified - SAP Fiori application developer
